   729→## Cost Analysis
   730→
   731→### Monthly Cost Estimate (Production)
   732→
   733→| Component | Configuration | Estimated Cost (USD) |
   734→|-----------|---------------|---------------------|
   735→| **DynamoDB** | On-demand, ~1000 writes/month, ~5000 reads/month | $2.50 |
   736→| **DynamoDB Global Tables** | Cross-region replication | $1.25 |
   737→| **Lambda (DLQ-Consumer)** | 256MB, ~500 invocations/month, 10s avg | $0.05 |
   738→| **Lambda (Replay)** | 512MB, ~100 invocations/month, 30s avg | $0.02 |
   739→| **Lambda (Stuck Recovery)** | 256MB, 2880 invocations/month (every 15 min) | $0.10 |
   740→| **Lambda (TTL Archiver)** | 256MB, ~50 invocations/month | $0.01 |
   741→| **API Gateway** | ~200 requests/month | $0.70 |
   742→| **KMS** | 1 key, ~2000 encrypt/decrypt ops | $1.03 |
   743→| **S3 (Archive)** | ~1GB/year, Glacier after 1 year | $0.01 |
   744→| **CloudWatch** | Logs, metrics, alarms | $3.00 |
   745→| **EventBridge** | ~100 events/month | $0.00 |
   746→| **WAF** | Basic rules, ~200 requests | $5.50 |
   747→| **VPC Endpoints** | DynamoDB, SQS, KMS (if not existing) | $21.60 |
   748→| **Total** | | **~$35.77/month** |
   749→
   750→### Cost Optimization Strategies
   751→
   752→1. **VPC Endpoints Sharing**: Use existing VPC endpoints if available (saves $21.60/month)
   753→2. **Reserved Concurrency**: Avoid over-provisioning Lambda concurrency
   754→3. **GSI Projections**: Use INCLUDE instead of ALL to reduce storage
   755→4. **S3 Lifecycle**: Aggressive transition to Glacier for archives
   756→5. **Alarm Consolidation**: Use composite alarms to reduce alarm count
   757→
   758→### Cost Monitoring
   759→
   760→```yaml
   761→FeeExemptionBudget:
   762→  Type: AWS::Budgets::Budget
   763→  Properties:
   764→    Budget:
   765→      BudgetName: fee-exemption-monthly
   766→      BudgetLimit:
   767→        Amount: 50
   768→        Unit: USD
   769→      TimeUnit: MONTHLY
   770→      BudgetType: COST
   771→      CostFilters:
   772→        TagKeyValue:
   773→          - "user:Application$fee-exemption"
   774→    NotificationsWithSubscribers:
   775→      - Notification:
   776→          NotificationType: ACTUAL
   777→          ComparisonOperator: GREATER_THAN
   778→          Threshold: 80
   779→        Subscribers:
   780→          - SubscriptionType: EMAIL
   781→            Address: squad-fenix@company.com
   782→```

   786→## API Versioning Strategy
   787→
   788→### URI Versioning
   789→
   790→```
   791→Base URL: https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1
   792→```
   793→
   794→### Version Lifecycle
   795→
   796→| Version | Status | End of Life |
   797→|---------|--------|-------------|
   798→| v1 | **Current** | - |
   799→| v2 (future) | Planned | - |
   800→
   801→### Breaking Change Policy
   802→
   803→1. **Non-breaking changes**: Add to current version
   804→   - New optional fields in responses
   805→   - New optional query parameters
   806→   - New endpoints
   807→
   808→2. **Breaking changes**: New major version
   809→   - Removing fields
   810→   - Changing field types
   811→   - Changing URL structure
   812→
   813→### Deprecation Process

   816→// Response headers for deprecated endpoints
   817→{

   821→}

   824→### API Specification (OpenAPI 3.1)
   825→
   826→```yaml
   827→openapi: 3.1.0
   828→info:
   829→  title: Fee Exemption Replay API
   830→  version: 1.0.0
   831→  description: API for managing fee exemption failure reprocessing
   832→servers:
   833→  - url: https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1
   834→    description: Production
   835→  - url: https://api.feeexemption.dev.internal/v1
   836→    description: Development
   837→
   838→paths:
   839→  /replay/cpf:
   840→    post:
   841→      summary: Replay specific CPFs
   842→      operationId: replayCpf
   843→      tags: [Replay]
   844→      security:
   845→        - ApiKeyAuth: []
   846→      requestBody:
   847→        required: true
   848→        content:
   849→          application/json:
   850→            schema:
   851→              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayCpfRequest'
   852→      responses:
   853→        '200':
   854→          description: Replay initiated
   855→          content:
   856→            application/json:
   857→              schema:
   858→                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayResponse'
   859→        '400':
   860→          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
   861→        '429':
   862→          $ref: '#/components/responses/TooManyRequests'
   863→
   864→  /replay/batch:
   865→    post:
   866→      summary: Replay batch of failures by criteria
   867→      operationId: replayBatch
   868→      tags: [Replay]
   869→      requestBody:
   870→        content:
   871→          application/json:
   872→            schema:
   873→              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayBatchRequest'
   874→      responses:
   875→        '200':
   876→          $ref: '#/components/responses/ReplayResponse'
   877→
   878→  /failures:
   879→    get:
   880→      summary: Query failure records
   881→      operationId: getFailures
   882→      tags: [Query]
   883→      parameters:
   884→        - name: cpf
   885→          in: query
   886→          schema:
   887→            type: string
   888→            pattern: '^\d{11}$'
   889→        - name: status
   890→          in: query
   891→          schema:
   892→            $ref: '#/components/schemas/FailureStatus'
   893→        - name: from
   894→          in: query
   895→          schema:
   896→            type: string
   897→            format: date
   898→        - name: to
   899→          in: query
   900→          schema:
   901→            type: string
   902→            format: date
   903→        - name: limit
   904→          in: query
   905→          schema:
   906→            type: integer
   907→            minimum: 1
   908→            maximum: 100
   909→            default: 50
   910→      responses:
   911→        '200':
   912→          description: Failure records
   913→          content:
   914→            application/json:
   915→              schema:
   916→                $ref: '#/components/schemas/FailuresResponse'
   917→
   918→  /health:
   919→    get:
   920→      summary: Health check
   921→      operationId: healthCheck
   922→      tags: [Operations]
   923→      responses:
   924→        '200':
   925→          description: Service healthy
   926→          content:
   927→            application/json:
   928→              schema:
   929→                $ref: '#/components/schemas/HealthResponse'
   930→
   931→components:
   932→  schemas:
   933→    ReplayCpfRequest:
   934→      type: object
   935→      required: [cpfs]
   936→      properties:
   937→        cpfs:

  1005→## Multi-Environment Strategy
  1006→
  1007→### Environment Matrix
  1008→
  1009→| Environment | Purpose | Data | CBILL Connection |
  1010→|-------------|---------|------|------------------|
  1011→| **dev** | Development & debugging | Synthetic | Mock server |
  1012→| **staging** | Integration testing | Anonymized prod copy | Staging CBILL |
  1013→| **prod** | Production | Real | Production CBILL |
  1014→
  1015→### Environment Parity
  1016→
  1017→```yaml
  1018→# All environments use same CloudFormation templates
  1019→Parameters:
  1020→  Environment:
  1021→    Type: String
  1022→    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, prod]
  1023→
  1024→Mappings:
  1025→  EnvironmentConfig:
  1026→    dev:
  1027→      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1028→      LambdaMemory: 256
  1029→      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "false"
  1030→      LogLevel: DEBUG
  1031→    staging:
  1032→      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1033→      LambdaMemory: 256
  1034→      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "true"
  1035→      LogLevel: INFO
  1036→    prod:
  1037→      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1038→      LambdaMemory: 256
  1039→      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "true"
  1040→      LogLevel: INFO
  1041→```
  1042→
  1043→### Data Handling by Environment
  1044→
  1045→```typescript
  1046→// Environment-specific CPF handling
  1047→const getCpfSalt = async (): Promise<string> => {
  1048→  const env = process.env.ENVIRONMENT;
  1049→
  1050→  if (env === 'dev') {
  1051→    return 'dev-static-salt';  // Deterministic for debugging
  1052→  }
  1053→
  1054→  return ssmClient.getParameter({
  1055→    Name: `/fee-exemption/${env}/salt`,
  1056→    WithDecryption: true
  1057→  });
  1058→};
  1059→
  1060→// Test data factory (dev/staging only)
  1061→const generateTestCpf = (): string => {
  1062→  if (process.env.ENVIRONMENT === 'prod') {
  1063→    throw new Error('Cannot generate test CPFs in production');
  1064→  }
  1065→
  1066→  // Generate valid CPF for testing
  1067→  return generateValidCpf();
  1068→};
  1069→```

  1073→## Migration Runbook
  1074→
  1075→### Pre-Migration Checklist
  1076→
  1077→- [ ] FeeExemption Lambda idempotency implemented and tested
  1078→- [ ] KMS key created with correct policies
  1079→- [ ] VPC endpoints configured (if required)
  1080→- [ ] IAM roles created and tested
  1081→- [ ] CloudWatch alarms configured
  1082→- [ ] Runbooks written and reviewed
  1083→- [ ] Team trained on new workflow
  1084→- [ ] Rollback plan tested in staging
  1085→
  1086→### Migration Steps
  1087→
  1088→#### Phase 0: Prerequisites (Week 1)
  1089→
  1090→```bash
  1091→# 1. Deploy KMS key
  1092→aws cloudformation deploy \
  1093→  --template-file infrastructure/kms.yml \
  1094→  --stack-name fee-exemption-kms-prod \
  1095→  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1096→
  1097→# 2. Deploy DynamoDB table (no consumers yet)
  1098→aws cloudformation deploy \
  1099→  --template-file infrastructure/dynamodb.yml \
  1100→  --stack-name fee-exemption-dynamodb-prod \
  1101→  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1102→
  1103→# 3. Verify FeeExemption Lambda has idempotency
  1104→aws lambda invoke \
  1105→  --function-name NTS-APP-Fintech-FeeExemption \
  1106→  --payload '{"test": true, "idempotency_key": "test-key"}' \
  1107→  response.json
  1108→
  1109→# 4. Create SSM parameters
  1110→aws ssm put-parameter \
  1111→  --name /fee-exemption/prod/salt \
  1112→  --type SecureString \
  1113→  --value "$(openssl rand -base64 32)"
  1114→```
  1115→
  1116→#### Phase 1: Canary Deployment (Week 2)
  1117→
  1118→```bash
  1119→# 1. Deploy DLQ-Consumer with 1% traffic
  1120→aws lambda create-event-source-mapping \
  1121→  --function-name fee-exemption-dlq-consumer-prod \
  1122→  --event-source-arn arn:aws:sqs:sa-east-1:123456789012:SQS-Isencao_mensalidade_DLQ \
  1123→  --batch-size 1 \
  1124→  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=1
  1125→
  1126→# 2. Monitor for 48 hours
  1127→# - Check CloudWatch Logs for errors
  1128→# - Verify DynamoDB records are created
  1129→# - Confirm no impact on existing Step Function
  1130→
  1131→# 3. If successful, increase to 100%
  1132→aws lambda update-event-source-mapping \
  1133→  --uuid <UUID> \
  1134→  --batch-size 10 \
  1135→  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=5
  1136→```
  1137→
  1138→#### Phase 2: Full Deployment (Week 3)
  1139→
  1140→```bash
  1141→# 1. Deploy Replay Lambda and API Gateway
  1142→aws cloudformation deploy \
  1143→  --template-file infrastructure/replay.yml \
  1144→  --stack-name fee-exemption-replay-prod \
  1145→  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1146→
  1147→# 2. Configure auto-replay schedule
  1148→aws events put-rule \
  1149→  --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod \
  1150→  --schedule-expression "cron(0 6 * * ? *)" \
  1151→  --state DISABLED  # Enable after validation
  1152→
  1153→# 3. Deploy stuck recovery Lambda
  1154→aws cloudformation deploy \
  1155→  --template-file infrastructure/stuck-recovery.yml \
  1156→  --stack-name fee-exemption-stuck-recovery-prod
  1157→
  1158→# 4. Modify Step Function to use EventBridge trigger
  1159→# (instead of direct DLQ consumption)
  1160→```
  1161→
  1162→#### Phase 3: Validation (Week 4)
  1163→
  1164→```bash
  1165→# 1. Test manual replay via API
  1166→curl -X POST https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1/replay/cpf \
  1167→  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  1168→  -d '{"cpfs": ["<test-cpf>"]}'
  1169→
  1170→# 2. Verify end-to-end flow
  1171→# - Check DynamoDB record status changed
  1172→# - Check SQS main queue received message
  1173→# - Confirm CBILL processed successfully
  1174→
  1175→# 3. Enable auto-replay
  1176→aws events enable-rule --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod
  1177→
  1178→# 4. Game day exercise
  1179→# - Simulate CBILL outage
  1180→# - Verify records persist in DynamoDB
  1181→# - Verify replay works after recovery
  1182→```
  1183→
  1184→### Rollback Procedure
  1185→
  1186→See **RB-007: Rollback** runbook in V2 (unchanged).
  1187→
  1188→---
  1189→
  1190→## Operational Runbooks (Updated)
  1191→
  1192→### Required Runbooks
  1193→
  1194→| ID | Name | Trigger |
  1195→|----|------|---------|
  1196→| RB-001 | High Failure Rate Response | > 10 failures/hour |
  1197→| RB-002 | Stale Records Handling | PENDING_RETRY > 7 days |
  1198→| RB-003 | Manual CPF Replay | Manual request |
  1199→| RB-004 | CBILL Outage Response | CBILL unavailable |
  1200→| RB-005 | Circuit Breaker Open | Auto-replay stopped |
  1201→| RB-006 | Stuck IN_PROGRESS Recovery | Records > 30 min |
  1202→| RB-007 | Full System Rollback | Critical failure |
  1203→| RB-008 | DR Failover | Regional outage (NEW) |
  1204→| RB-009 | SLO Breach Response | Error budget exhausted (NEW) |
  1205→| RB-010 | Archive Data Retrieval | Compliance request (NEW) |
  1206→
  1207→### RB-009: SLO Breach Response (NEW)
  1208→
  1209→```markdown
  1210→## SLO Breach Response
  1211→
  1212→### Trigger
  1213→- Error budget for any SLO < 10% remaining
  1214→- CloudWatch Alarm: fee-exemption-slo-breach
  1215→
  1216→### Severity Assessment
  1217→
  1218→| Budget Remaining | Severity | Response Time |
  1219→|-----------------|----------|---------------|
  1220→| < 10% | P2 | 4 hours |
  1221→| < 5% | P1 | 1 hour |
  1222→| Exhausted | P0 | Immediate |
  1223→
  1224→### Response Steps
  1225→
  1226→1. **Identify breaching SLO** (5 min)
  1227→   - Check CloudWatch Dashboard for affected metric
  1228→   - Review recent changes (deployments, config changes)
  1229→
  1230→2. **Assess impact** (10 min)
  1231→   - Is the breach ongoing or historical?
  1232→   - How many customers affected?
  1233→
  1234→3. **Mitigation actions by SLO**:
  1235→
  1236→   **Availability < 99.9%**:
  1237→   - Check Lambda error logs
  1238→   - Verify DynamoDB/SQS health
  1239→   - Consider DR failover if regional issue
  1240→
  1241→   **Durability < 99.999%**:
  1242→   - Pause DLQ-Consumer if still losing records
  1243→   - Check for DynamoDB write failures
  1244→   - Review batch failure handling
  1245→
  1246→   **Replay Latency > 5 min (p95)**:
  1247→   - Check DynamoDB latency metrics
  1248→   - Review Lambda cold starts
  1249→   - Consider increasing provisioned concurrency
  1250→
  1251→   **Resolution Time > 48h**:
  1252→   - Investigate stuck records
  1253→   - Check CBILL availability
  1254→   - Review error classification accuracy
  1255→
  1256→   **Replay Success < 98%**:
  1257→   - Analyze failure reasons
  1258→   - Check circuit breaker state
  1259→   - Review CBILL error responses
  1260→
  1261→4. **Document incident** (post-resolution)
  1262→   - Update incident log
  1263→   - Schedule post-mortem if P0/P1
  1264→   - Update runbooks if needed
  1265→
  1266→### Escalation
  1267→
  1268→| Severity | Escalation Path |
  1269→|----------|-----------------|
  1270→| P2 | Squad Fenix on-call |
  1271→| P1 | Squad Fenix lead + Platform team |
  1272→| P0 | VP Engineering + All hands |
  1273→```
  1274→
  1275→### RB-010: Archive Data Retrieval (NEW)
  1276→
  1277→```markdown
  1278→## Archive Data Retrieval
  1279→
  1280→### Trigger
  1281→- LGPD data subject request
  1282→- Legal/compliance inquiry
  1283→- Audit request
  1284→
  1285→### Access Control
  1286→Only the following roles can request archive retrieval:
  1287→- Data Protection Officer
  1288→- Legal Team
  1289→- Compliance Team
  1290→
  1291→### Retrieval Steps
  1292→
  1293→1. **Validate request** (1 day SLA)
  1294→   - Confirm requestor authorization
  1295→   - Verify CPF format
  1296→   - Log request for audit trail
  1297→
  1298→2. **Determine data location**
  1299→   - If < 90 days: Query DynamoDB directly
  1300→   - If 90-365 days: Query S3 Standard/IA
  1301→   - If > 365 days: Initiate Glacier restore
  1302→
  1303→3. **Glacier retrieval** (if applicable)
  1304→   ```bash
  1305→   # Initiate restore (Expedited: 1-5 min, Standard: 3-5 hours)
  1306→   aws s3api restore-object \
  1307→     --bucket fee-exemption-archive-123456789012-prod \
  1308→     --key "year=2025/month=01/cpf_token=abc123.parquet" \
  1309→     --restore-request '{"Days": 7, "GlacierJobParameters": {"Tier": "Standard"}}'
  1310→   ```
  1311→
  1312→4. **Extract requested data**
  1313→   ```bash
  1314→   # Query with Athena (S3)
  1315→   aws athena start-query-execution \
  1316→     --query-string "SELECT * FROM fee_exemption_archive WHERE cpf_token = 'abc123'" \
  1317→     --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://query-results/
  1318→   ```
  1319→
  1320→5. **Prepare response**
  1321→   - Decrypt CPF using KMS
  1322→   - Format according to LGPD requirements
  1323→   - Send via secure channel (never email)
  1324→
  1325→6. **Log completion**
  1326→   - Record retrieval in audit log
  1327→   - Notify requestor
  1328→   - Delete temporary files
  1329→```
  1330→
  1331→---
  1332→
  1333→## Component Specifications
  1334→
  1335→*(Retained from V2 with minor updates)*
  1336→
  1337→### Lambda Functions Summary
  1338→
  1339→| Function | Memory | Timeout | VPC | Concurrency |
  1340→|----------|--------|---------|-----|-------------|
  1341→| DLQ-Consumer | 256 MB | 30s | Yes | 5 (reserved) |
  1342→| Replay | 512 MB | 5 min | Yes | 10 (reserved) |
  1343→| Stuck Recovery | 256 MB | 60s | Yes | 1 |
  1344→| TTL Archiver | 256 MB | 60s | No | 5 |
  1345→| Health Check | 128 MB | 10s | Yes | 2 |
  1346→
  1347→### Memory Tuning Guidance
  1348→
  1349→Based on Lambda Power Tuning analysis:
  1350→
  1351→| Function | Optimal Memory | Cost/Invocation | Duration (avg) |
  1352→|----------|----------------|-----------------|----------------|
  1353→| DLQ-Consumer | 256 MB | $0.000004 | 800ms |
  1354→| Replay | 512 MB | $0.000025 | 2.5s |
  1355→| Stuck Recovery | 256 MB | $0.000003 | 500ms |
  1356→
  1357→---
  1358→
  1359→## Appendix: Complete CloudFormation Template Structure
  1360→
  1361→```
  1362→infrastructure/
  1363→├── main.yml                    # Parent stack
  1364→├── kms.yml                     # KMS key for CPF encryption
  1365→├── dynamodb.yml                # Global table with indexes
  1366→├── lambdas/
  1367→│   ├── dlq-consumer.yml
  1368→│   ├── replay.yml
  1369→│   ├── stuck-recovery.yml
  1370→│   └── ttl-archiver.yml
  1371→├── api-gateway.yml             # API Gateway with WAF
  1372→├── eventbridge.yml             # Schedules and rules
  1373→├── monitoring/
  1374→│   ├── alarms.yml
  1375→│   ├── dashboard.yml
  1376→│   └── slo-tracking.yml
  1377→├── networking/
  1378→│   └── vpc-endpoints.yml
  1379→├── s3.yml                      # Archive bucket
  1380→└── iam/
  1381→    ├── dlq-consumer-role.yml
  1382→    ├── replay-role.yml
  1383→    └── service-roles.yml
  1384→```
  1385→
  1386→---
  1387→
  1388→## Appendix: Decision Log
  1389→
  1390→| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered |
  1391→|------|----------|-----------|------------------------|
  1392→| 2026-02-03 | Use DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless scaling, no connection pooling needed | Aurora Serverless (higher cost) |
  1393→| 2026-02-03 | CPF tokenization over encryption-only | Faster lookups without decryption | Field-level encryption (slower queries) |
  1394→| 2026-02-04 | Global Tables for DR | Automatic replication, <1min RPO | Manual cross-region backup (higher RPO) |
  1395→| 2026-02-04 | S3 for archival over DynamoDB | Cost-effective long-term storage | DynamoDB (10x cost for cold data) |
  1396→| 2026-02-04 | URI versioning over header | Simpler debugging, cache-friendly | Accept header (more RESTful but complex) |
  1397→| 2026-02-04 | Parquet for archives | Columnar format for efficient queries | JSON (simpler but larger) |
  1398→
  1399→---
  1400→
  1401→*Document Version: 3.0*
  1402→*Last Updated: 2026-02-04*
  1403→*Authors: Architecture Review (REV003)*
  1404→*Reviewers: Pending*
  1405→

# Solution V3: CPF Queue Automation for Fee Exemption Reprocessing

> Architecture Design Document - Revision 4 (Production-Ready)

## Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| V0 | 2026-02-03 | Initial | Original proposal |
| V1 | 2026-02-03 | REV002 | Detailed architecture |
| V2 | 2026-02-04 | REV002 | Security, SRE, DevOps fixes |
| V3 | 2026-02-04 | REV003 | Production readiness, DR, SLOs, Testing |

---

## Executive Summary

This document presents a **production-ready architecture** for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings to CBILL. **V3 builds upon V2's security and reliability improvements** by adding:

1. **Disaster Recovery Strategy** with cross-region failover
2. **Defined SLOs/SLIs** for measurable service quality
3. **Comprehensive Testing Strategy** (unit, integration, load, chaos)
4. **Cost Analysis and Optimization**
5. **Data Lifecycle Management** with archival for compliance
6. **Multi-Environment Strategy** with environment parity
7. **Migration Runbook** for safe production deployment
8. **API Versioning** for backward compatibility

### Key Additions from V2

| Aspect | V2 | V3 |
|--------|----|----|
| Disaster Recovery | Not specified | Cross-region DynamoDB Global Tables |
| SLOs/SLIs | Target metrics only | Formal SLOs with error budgets |
| Testing | Not specified | 4-layer testing pyramid |
| Cost Analysis | Not specified | Full TCO breakdown |
| Data Archival | TTL only | S3 Glacier archival |
| API Versioning | None | URI versioning (/v1/) |
| Migration | Phases only | Detailed runbook with canary |

---

## Problem Statement

*(Unchanged from V2)*

### Current Pain Points

1. **Manual Reprocessing**: Requires opening tickets with CBILL team
2. **TTL Data Loss**: Messages lost after 14 days in DLQ
3. **No Selective Replay**: Native SQS Redrive moves ALL messages, not specific CPFs
4. **No Audit Trail**: No historical record of failures and retry attempts
5. **Limited Visibility**: Monitoring only via AWS Console

---

## Architecture Overview

### Design Principles

*(Retained from V2 + additions)*

1. **Durability Over Convenience**: Persist failures immediately
2. **Selective Replay**: CPF-level granularity for reprocessing
3. **Idempotency**: Safe retries without duplicate promotions (MANDATORY)
4. **Observability**: Full audit trail with distributed tracing
5. **Minimal Disruption**: Extend existing infrastructure
6. **LGPD Compliance**: CPF tokenization and encryption
7. **Fail-Safe Operations**: Circuit breakers and graceful degradation
8. **Multi-Region Resilience**: DR with defined RTO/RPO (NEW)
9. **Testability**: Design for automated testing at all layers (NEW)
10. **Cost Efficiency**: Right-sized resources with monitoring (NEW)

### High-Level Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              EXISTING FLOW                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                 │
│  Data Lake (Afinz)                                                              │
│       │                                                                         │
│       ▼                                                                         │
│  ┌─────────────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────────────────┐            │
│  │ SQS-Isenção_mensalidade │───▶│ Lambda: FeeExemption            │            │
│  └─────────────────────────┘    │ (NTS-APP-Fintech-FeeExemption)  │            │
│           ▲                     │ + Idempotency Check (Phase 0)   │            │
│           │                     └─────────────────┬───────────────┘            │
│           │                                       │                             │
│           │                              GraphQL (3 calls)                      │
│           │                                       │                             │
│           │                                       ▼                             │
│           │                              ┌───────────────┐                      │
│           │                              │  CBILL (OnPrem)│                      │
│           │                              └───────┬───────┘                      │
│           │                                      │                              │
│           │                             Success / Error                         │
│           │                                      │                              │
└───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┘
            │                                      │
            │                              (After X retries)
            │                                      │
┌───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┐
│           │              NEW COMPONENTS (V3)     ▼                              │
│           │                     ┌────────────────────────────┐                  │
│           │                     │  SQS-Isenção_mensalidade_DLQ│                  │
│           │                     └────────────┬───────────────┘                  │
│           │                                  │                                  │
│           │                      (SOLE CONSUMER)                                │
│           │                                  ▼                                  │
│           │              ┌───────────────────────────────────┐                  │
│           │              │ Lambda: DLQ-Consumer (VPC)        │                  │
│           │              │ + ReportBatchItemFailures         │                  │
│           │              │ + Atomic Conditional Writes       │                  │
│           │              │ + Error Classification            │                  │
│           │              └───────────────────┬───────────────┘                  │
│           │                                  │                                  │
│           │                    ┌─────────────┴─────────────┐                    │
│           │                    │                           │                    │
│           │                    ▼                           ▼                    │
│           │      ┌──────────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐          │
│           │      │  DynamoDB            │    │ EventBridge          │          │
│           │      │  fee_exemption_      │    │ 'failure.recorded'   │          │
│           │      │  failures            │    └──────────┬───────────┘          │
│           │      │  [Global Table]      │               │                      │

│           │         │               │                                          │
│           │         ▼               ▼                                          │
│           │   ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────────┐                                  │
│           │   │ API GW   │   │ EventBridge  │                                  │

│           │   │ + WAF    │   │ + Circuit    │                                  │
│           │   │ + Rate   │   │   Breaker    │                                  │
│           │   └────┬─────┘   └──────┬───────┘                                  │
│           │        │                │                                           │
│           │        └────────┬───────┘                                           │
│           │                 ▼                                                   │
│           │      ┌──────────────────────┐                                       │
│           │      │ Lambda: Replay (VPC) │                                       │
│           │      │ + Atomic Status      │                                       │
│           │      │ + Canary Metrics     │                                       │
│           │      └──────────┬───────────┘                                       │
│           │                 │                                                   │
│           └─────────────────┘                                                   │
│                                                                                 │
│           ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐           │
│           │                 SUPPORTING SERVICES                      │           │
│           ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤           │
│           │  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────┐ │           │
│           │  │ Stuck Recovery │  │ TTL Archiver   │  │ Health     │ │           │
│           │  │ Lambda (15min) │  │ Lambda (daily) │  │ Dashboard  │ │           │
│           │  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────┘ │           │
│           └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Service Level Objectives (SLOs)

### Defined SLOs

| SLO | Target | Measurement Window | Error Budget |
|-----|--------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Availability** | 99.9% | Monthly | 43.8 min downtime |
| **Data Durability** | 99.999% | Monthly | < 1 record lost per 100,000 |
| **Replay Latency** | p95 < 5 min | Daily | 5% requests exceed |
| **Time to Resolution** | < 48 hours | Weekly | 95% resolved within window |
| **Successful Replay Rate** | > 98% | Weekly | 2% failure budget |

### Service Level Indicators (SLIs)

```typescript
interface SLI {
  availability: number;      // (successful_requests / total_requests) * 100
  durability: number;        // (persisted_records / dlq_messages_received) * 100
  replayLatency: number;     // time from PENDING_RETRY to SQS publish (p95)
  resolutionTime: number;    // time from first failure to RESOLVED status (hours)
  replaySuccessRate: number; // (successful_replays / attempted_replays) * 100
}
```

### Alerting Thresholds

| SLO | Warning (80% budget) | Critical (90% budget) |
|-----|---------------------|----------------------|
| Availability | 99.92% | 99.91% |
| Durability | 99.9998% | 99.9996% |
| Replay Latency | p95 > 4 min | p95 > 4.5 min |
| Resolution Time | > 40 hours | > 44 hours |
| Replay Success | < 98.4% | < 98.2% |

---

## Disaster Recovery Strategy

### Recovery Objectives

| Metric | Target | Strategy |
|--------|--------|----------|
| **RTO** (Recovery Time Objective) | < 30 minutes | Automated failover |
| **RPO** (Recovery Point Objective) | < 1 minute | DynamoDB Global Tables |

### Multi-Region Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           PRIMARY REGION (sa-east-1)                        │
│                                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐    │
│  │ DLQ-Consumer│───▶│ DynamoDB            │◄──▶│ Replay Lambda       │    │
│  │ Lambda      │    │ fee_exemption_      │    │ + API Gateway       │    │
│  └─────────────┘    │ failures            │    └─────────────────────┘    │
│                     │ [ACTIVE]            │                                │
│                     └──────────┬──────────┘                                │
│                                │                                            │
│                    Global Table Replication                                 │
│                                │                                            │
└────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                 │
                    ┌────────────▼────────────┐
                    │  AWS Global Tables      │
                    │  (Automatic Sync)       │
                    └────────────┬────────────┘
                                 │
┌────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                │                                            │
│                           SECONDARY REGION (us-east-1)                      │
│                                │                                            │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────▼───────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐    │
│  │ DLQ-Consumer│    │ DynamoDB            │    │ Replay Lambda       │    │
│  │ Lambda      │    │ fee_exemption_      │◄──▶│ + API Gateway       │    │
│  │ [STANDBY]   │    │ failures            │    │ [STANDBY]           │    │
│  └─────────────┘    │ [READ REPLICA]      │    └─────────────────────┘    │
│                     └─────────────────────┘                                │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Failover Procedure

```markdown
## DR Failover Runbook (RB-008)

### Trigger Conditions
- Primary region outage > 5 minutes
- DynamoDB availability < 99.5% for 10 minutes
- Network connectivity to CBILL lost (and CBILL supports multi-region)

### Automated Failover (Route 53 Health Checks)

1. Route 53 detects API Gateway unhealthy (3 consecutive failures)
2. Traffic automatically routed to us-east-1 endpoint
3. CloudWatch alarm triggers SNS notification

### Manual Failover (if automated fails)

1. **Verify outage** (2 min)
   ```bash
   aws dynamodb describe-table \
     --table-name fee_exemption_failures_prod \
     --region sa-east-1
   ```

2. **Enable secondary region Lambdas** (3 min)
   ```bash
   aws lambda update-function-configuration \
     --function-name fee-exemption-dlq-consumer \
     --environment "Variables={ACTIVE_REGION=us-east-1}" \
     --region us-east-1

   aws lambda update-event-source-mapping \
     --uuid <DR_EVENT_SOURCE_UUID> \
     --enabled \
     --region us-east-1
   ```

3. **Update Route 53** (2 min)
   ```bash
   aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
     --hosted-zone-id <ZONE_ID> \
     --change-batch file://dr-failover.json
   ```

4. **Verify** (5 min)
   - Confirm DynamoDB reads in us-east-1
   - Test API endpoint responds
   - Monitor CloudWatch for errors

### Failback Procedure
1. Confirm primary region recovered
2. Verify DynamoDB replication caught up (LastSyncDateTime)
3. Reverse DNS change
4. Disable secondary Lambdas
```

## Data Lifecycle Management

### Lifecycle Stages

```
┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   ACTIVE    │────▶│   WARM      │────▶│   COLD      │────▶│   ARCHIVE   │
│  0-30 days  │     │  31-90 days │     │ 91-365 days │     │  > 365 days │
│  DynamoDB   │     │  DynamoDB   │     │  S3 Standard│     │  S3 Glacier │
└─────────────┘     └─────────────┘     └─────────────┘     └─────────────┘
      │                   │                   │                    │
      └───────────────────┴───────────────────┴────────────────────┘
                          TTL Archiver Lambda
```

### TTL Archiver Lambda (NEW)

**Purpose**: Archive records before TTL deletion for LGPD compliance (5-year retention).

**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (filtering for TTL deletions)

```typescript
interface ArchiveRecord {
  cpf_token: string;
  cpf_encrypted: string;
  created_at: string;
  resolved_at: string | null;
  final_status: string;
  error_codes: string[];      // Aggregated error codes
  total_retry_count: number;
  archived_at: string;
}

const archiveHandler = async (event: DynamoDBStreamEvent): Promise<void> => {
  const recordsToArchive: ArchiveRecord[] = [];

  for (const record of event.Records) {
    // Filter for TTL deletions (userIdentity contains principalId 'dynamodb.amazonaws.com')
    if (record.eventName === 'REMOVE' &&
        record.userIdentity?.principalId === 'dynamodb.amazonaws.com') {

      const oldImage = unmarshall(record.dynamodb.OldImage);
      recordsToArchive.push({
        cpf_token: oldImage.cpf_token,
        cpf_encrypted: oldImage.cpf_encrypted,
        created_at: oldImage.created_at,
        resolved_at: oldImage.status === 'RESOLVED' ? oldImage.last_retry_at : null,
        final_status: oldImage.status,
        error_codes: [oldImage.error_code],
        total_retry_count: oldImage.total_retry_count,
        archived_at: new Date().toISOString()
      });
    }
  }

  if (recordsToArchive.length > 0) {
    // Write to S3 in Parquet format for efficient querying
    await writeToS3Archive(recordsToArchive);
  }
};
```

### S3 Archive Configuration

```yaml
FeeExemptionArchiveBucket:
  Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
  Properties:
    BucketName: !Sub 'fee-exemption-archive-${AWS::AccountId}-${Environment}'
    VersioningConfiguration:
      Status: Enabled
    BucketEncryption:
      ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
        - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
            SSEAlgorithm: aws:kms
            KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref FeeExemptionKMSKey
    LifecycleConfiguration:
      Rules:
        - Id: TransitionToGlacier
          Status: Enabled
          Transitions:
            - StorageClass: STANDARD_IA
              TransitionInDays: 90
            - StorageClass: GLACIER
              TransitionInDays: 365
            - StorageClass: DEEP_ARCHIVE
              TransitionInDays: 730
          ExpirationInDays: 2190  # 6 years (LGPD + 1 year buffer)
    PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
      BlockPublicAcls: true
      BlockPublicPolicy: true
      IgnorePublicAcls: true
      RestrictPublicBuckets: true
    Tags:
      - Key: DataClassification
        Value: PII-Encrypted
      - Key: Compliance
        Value: LGPD
```

### 1. Unit Tests

**Coverage Target**: 80% line coverage

**Frameworks**: Jest, @aws-sdk/client-mock

```typescript
// Example: DLQ-Consumer error classification
describe('classifyError', () => {
  it('should classify TIMEOUT as retryable', () => {
    const result = classifyError('GraphQL TIMEOUT after 30s');
    expect(result).toEqual({ code: 'CBILL_TIMEOUT', retryable: true });
  });

  it('should classify NRC_NOT_FOUND as non-retryable', () => {
    const result = classifyError('NRC_NOT_FOUND for CPF');
    expect(result).toEqual({ code: 'NRC_NOT_FOUND', retryable: false });
  });

  it('should classify unknown errors as retryable', () => {
    const result = classifyError('Something unexpected happened');
    expect(result).toEqual({ code: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true });
  });
});

// Example: CPF tokenization
describe('tokenizeCpf', () => {
  it('should produce consistent hash', () => {
    const cpf = '12345678901';
    const salt = 'test-salt';
    const token1 = tokenizeCpf(cpf, salt);
    const token2 = tokenizeCpf(cpf, salt);
    expect(token1).toBe(token2);
  });

  it('should produce different hash with different salt', () => {
    const cpf = '12345678901';
    const token1 = tokenizeCpf(cpf, 'salt1');
    const token2 = tokenizeCpf(cpf, 'salt2');
    expect(token1).not.toBe(token2);
  });
});
```

```typescript
// Component test example
describe('DLQ-Consumer Lambda (Component)', () => {
  let dynamoClient: DynamoDBDocumentClient;
  let sqsClient: SQSClient;

  beforeAll(async () => {
    dynamoClient = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(new DynamoDBClient({
      endpoint: 'http://localhost:4566'
    }));
    // Create table...
  });

  it('should persist failure record from SQS message', async () => {
    const event = createSQSEvent({
      cpf: '12345678901',
      error: 'CBILL_TIMEOUT'
    });

    await handler(event, {} as Context);

    const result = await dynamoClient.send(new QueryCommand({
      TableName: 'fee_exemption_failures_test',
      KeyConditionExpression: 'cpf_token = :token',
      ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':token': tokenizeCpf('12345678901') }
    }));

    expect(result.Items).toHaveLength(1);
    expect(result.Items[0].status).toBe('PENDING_RETRY');
  });

  it('should handle duplicate messages idempotently', async () => {
    const event = createSQSEvent({
      cpf: '12345678901',
      error: 'CBILL_TIMEOUT',
      messageId: 'same-id'
    });

    await handler(event, {} as Context);
    await handler(event, {} as Context);  // Duplicate

    const result = await dynamoClient.send(new QueryCommand({/*...*/}));
    expect(result.Items).toHaveLength(1);  // Still one record
  });
});
```

```typescript
// Integration test (runs against dev AWS account)
describe('Replay Flow (Integration)', () => {
  it('should move record from DynamoDB to SQS', async () => {
    // Setup: Insert a PENDING_RETRY record
    const cpf = generateTestCpf();
    await insertTestFailure(cpf, 'PENDING_RETRY');

    // Act: Call replay API

    // Verify: Check SQS received message

    expect(messages.some(m => m.Body.includes(cpf))).toBe(true);

    // Verify: DynamoDB status updated

    expect(record.status).toBe('IN_PROGRESS');
  });
});

### 4. End-to-End / Chaos Tests

**Purpose**: Validate system resilience under failure conditions

**Framework**: AWS Fault Injection Simulator (FIS)

```yaml
FeeExemptionChaosExperiment:
  Type: AWS::FIS::ExperimentTemplate
  Properties:
    Description: Test DLQ-Consumer behavior during DynamoDB latency
    Actions:
      InjectLatency:
        ActionId: aws:dynamodb:disrupt-dynamodb-requests
        Parameters:
          duration: PT5M
          rate: 50
          latencyMs: 500
        Targets:
          DynamoDBTables: fee_exemption_failures_prod
    StopConditions:
      - Source: aws:cloudwatch:alarm
        Value: !GetAtt DLQConsumerHighErrorRateAlarm.Arn
    RoleArn: !GetAtt FISRole.Arn
    Tags:
      Name: fee-exemption-dynamodb-latency
```

**Chaos Scenarios**:

| Scenario | Injection | Expected Behavior |
|----------|-----------|-------------------|
| DynamoDB Latency | 500ms added | SQS messages retry, no data loss |
| DynamoDB Throttling | 50% requests throttled | Exponential backoff, eventual success |
| Lambda Timeout | Reduce timeout to 5s | ReportBatchItemFailures works |
| KMS Unavailable | Block KMS calls | Records not written, alerts fire |
| SQS SendMessage Failure | Block main queue | Status not changed to IN_PROGRESS |

## Cost Analysis

### Monthly Cost Estimate (Production)

| Component | Configuration | Estimated Cost (USD) |
|-----------|---------------|---------------------|
| **DynamoDB** | On-demand, ~1000 writes/month, ~5000 reads/month | $2.50 |
| **DynamoDB Global Tables** | Cross-region replication | $1.25 |
| **Lambda (DLQ-Consumer)** | 256MB, ~500 invocations/month, 10s avg | $0.05 |
| **Lambda (Replay)** | 512MB, ~100 invocations/month, 30s avg | $0.02 |
| **Lambda (Stuck Recovery)** | 256MB, 2880 invocations/month (every 15 min) | $0.10 |
| **Lambda (TTL Archiver)** | 256MB, ~50 invocations/month | $0.01 |
| **API Gateway** | ~200 requests/month | $0.70 |
| **KMS** | 1 key, ~2000 encrypt/decrypt ops | $1.03 |
| **S3 (Archive)** | ~1GB/year, Glacier after 1 year | $0.01 |
| **CloudWatch** | Logs, metrics, alarms | $3.00 |
| **EventBridge** | ~100 events/month | $0.00 |
| **WAF** | Basic rules, ~200 requests | $5.50 |
| **VPC Endpoints** | DynamoDB, SQS, KMS (if not existing) | $21.60 |
| **Total** | | **~$35.77/month** |

### Cost Optimization Strategies

1. **VPC Endpoints Sharing**: Use existing VPC endpoints if available (saves $21.60/month)
2. **Reserved Concurrency**: Avoid over-provisioning Lambda concurrency
3. **GSI Projections**: Use INCLUDE instead of ALL to reduce storage
4. **S3 Lifecycle**: Aggressive transition to Glacier for archives
5. **Alarm Consolidation**: Use composite alarms to reduce alarm count

### Cost Monitoring

## API Versioning Strategy

### URI Versioning

```
Base URL: https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1
```

### Version Lifecycle

| Version | Status | End of Life |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| v1 | **Current** | - |
| v2 (future) | Planned | - |

### Breaking Change Policy

1. **Non-breaking changes**: Add to current version
   - New optional fields in responses
   - New optional query parameters
   - New endpoints

2. **Breaking changes**: New major version
   - Removing fields
   - Changing field types
   - Changing URL structure

### Deprecation Process

```typescript

}

### API Specification (OpenAPI 3.1)

```yaml
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Fee Exemption Replay API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing fee exemption failure reprocessing
servers:
  - url: https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1
    description: Production
  - url: https://api.feeexemption.dev.internal/v1
    description: Development

paths:
  /replay/cpf:
    post:
      summary: Replay specific CPFs
      operationId: replayCpf
      tags: [Replay]
      security:
        - ApiKeyAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayCpfRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Replay initiated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '429':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/TooManyRequests'

  /replay/batch:
    post:
      summary: Replay batch of failures by criteria
      operationId: replayBatch
      tags: [Replay]
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayBatchRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/ReplayResponse'

  /failures:
    get:
      summary: Query failure records
      operationId: getFailures
      tags: [Query]
      parameters:
        - name: cpf
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            pattern: '^\d{11}$'
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/FailureStatus'
        - name: from
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
        - name: to
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            minimum: 1
            maximum: 100
            default: 50
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Failure records
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/FailuresResponse'

  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      operationId: healthCheck
      tags: [Operations]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/HealthResponse'

components:
  schemas:
    ReplayCpfRequest:
      type: object
      required: [cpfs]
      properties:
        cpfs:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
            pattern: '^\d{11}$'
          minItems: 1
          maxItems: 100

    ReplayBatchRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/FailureStatus'
        limit:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1
          maximum: 500
          default: 100
        older_than_hours:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1
          maximum: 168
          default: 24

    ReplayResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        queued:
          type: integer
        skipped:
          type: integer
        errors:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReplayError'

    FailureStatus:
      type: string
      enum: [PENDING_RETRY, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, EXPIRED, PERMANENT_FAILURE]

    HealthResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [healthy, degraded, unhealthy]
        components:
          type: object
          properties:
            dynamodb:
              type: string
            sqs:
              type: string
            cbill:
              type: string
        timestamp:
          type: string
          format: date-time

  securitySchemes:
    ApiKeyAuth:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: Authorization
```

```typescript
// Environment-specific CPF handling
const getCpfSalt = async (): Promise<string> => {
  const env = process.env.ENVIRONMENT;

  if (env === 'dev') {
    return 'dev-static-salt';  // Deterministic for debugging
  }

  return ssmClient.getParameter({
    Name: `/fee-exemption/${env}/salt`,
    WithDecryption: true
  });
};

// Test data factory (dev/staging only)
const generateTestCpf = (): string => {
  if (process.env.ENVIRONMENT === 'prod') {
    throw new Error('Cannot generate test CPFs in production');
  }

  // Generate valid CPF for testing
  return generateValidCpf();
};

## Migration Runbook

### Pre-Migration Checklist

- [ ] FeeExemption Lambda idempotency implemented and tested
- [ ] KMS key created with correct policies
- [ ] VPC endpoints configured (if required)
- [ ] IAM roles created and tested
- [ ] CloudWatch alarms configured
- [ ] Runbooks written and reviewed
- [ ] Team trained on new workflow
- [ ] Rollback plan tested in staging

### Migration Steps

#### Phase 0: Prerequisites (Week 1)

```bash
# 1. Deploy KMS key
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file infrastructure/kms.yml \
  --stack-name fee-exemption-kms-prod \
  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod

# 2. Deploy DynamoDB table (no consumers yet)
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file infrastructure/dynamodb.yml \
  --stack-name fee-exemption-dynamodb-prod \
  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod

# 3. Verify FeeExemption Lambda has idempotency
aws lambda invoke \
  --function-name NTS-APP-Fintech-FeeExemption \
  --payload '{"test": true, "idempotency_key": "test-key"}' \
  response.json

# 4. Create SSM parameters
aws ssm put-parameter \
  --name /fee-exemption/prod/salt \
  --type SecureString \
  --value "$(openssl rand -base64 32)"
```

#### Phase 1: Canary Deployment (Week 2)

```bash
# 1. Deploy DLQ-Consumer with 1% traffic
aws lambda create-event-source-mapping \
  --function-name fee-exemption-dlq-consumer-prod \
  --event-source-arn arn:aws:sqs:sa-east-1:123456789012:SQS-Isencao_mensalidade_DLQ \
  --batch-size 1 \
  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=1

# 2. Monitor for 48 hours
# - Check CloudWatch Logs for errors
# - Verify DynamoDB records are created
# - Confirm no impact on existing Step Function

# 3. If successful, increase to 100%
aws lambda update-event-source-mapping \
  --uuid <UUID> \
  --batch-size 10 \
  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=5
```

#### Phase 2: Full Deployment (Week 3)

```bash
# 1. Deploy Replay Lambda and API Gateway
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file infrastructure/replay.yml \
  --stack-name fee-exemption-replay-prod \
  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod

# 2. Configure auto-replay schedule
aws events put-rule \
  --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod \
  --schedule-expression "cron(0 6 * * ? *)" \
  --state DISABLED  # Enable after validation

# 3. Deploy stuck recovery Lambda
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file infrastructure/stuck-recovery.yml \
  --stack-name fee-exemption-stuck-recovery-prod

# 4. Modify Step Function to use EventBridge trigger
# (instead of direct DLQ consumption)
```

#### Phase 3: Validation (Week 4)

```bash
# 1. Test manual replay via API
curl -X POST https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1/replay/cpf \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"cpfs": ["<test-cpf>"]}'

# 2. Verify end-to-end flow
# - Check DynamoDB record status changed
# - Check SQS main queue received message
# - Confirm CBILL processed successfully

# 3. Enable auto-replay
aws events enable-rule --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod

# 4. Game day exercise
# - Simulate CBILL outage
# - Verify records persist in DynamoDB
# - Verify replay works after recovery
```

| ID | Name | Trigger |

| RB-001 | High Failure Rate Response | > 10 failures/hour |
| RB-002 | Stale Records Handling | PENDING_RETRY > 7 days |
| RB-003 | Manual CPF Replay | Manual request |
| RB-004 | CBILL Outage Response | CBILL unavailable |
| RB-005 | Circuit Breaker Open | Auto-replay stopped |
| RB-006 | Stuck IN_PROGRESS Recovery | Records > 30 min |
| RB-007 | Full System Rollback | Critical failure |
| RB-008 | DR Failover | Regional outage (NEW) |
| RB-009 | SLO Breach Response | Error budget exhausted (NEW) |
| RB-010 | Archive Data Retrieval | Compliance request (NEW) |

### RB-009: SLO Breach Response (NEW)

```markdown
## SLO Breach Response

### Trigger
- Error budget for any SLO < 10% remaining
- CloudWatch Alarm: fee-exemption-slo-breach

### Severity Assessment

| Budget Remaining | Severity | Response Time |
|-----------------|----------|---------------|
| < 10% | P2 | 4 hours |
| < 5% | P1 | 1 hour |
| Exhausted | P0 | Immediate |

### Response Steps

1. **Identify breaching SLO** (5 min)
   - Check CloudWatch Dashboard for affected metric
   - Review recent changes (deployments, config changes)

2. **Assess impact** (10 min)
   - Is the breach ongoing or historical?
   - How many customers affected?

3. **Mitigation actions by SLO**:

   **Availability < 99.9%**:
   - Check Lambda error logs
   - Verify DynamoDB/SQS health
   - Consider DR failover if regional issue

   **Durability < 99.999%**:
   - Pause DLQ-Consumer if still losing records
   - Check for DynamoDB write failures
   - Review batch failure handling

   **Replay Latency > 5 min (p95)**:
   - Check DynamoDB latency metrics
   - Review Lambda cold starts
   - Consider increasing provisioned concurrency

   **Resolution Time > 48h**:
   - Investigate stuck records
   - Check CBILL availability
   - Review error classification accuracy

   **Replay Success < 98%**:
   - Analyze failure reasons
   - Check circuit breaker state
   - Review CBILL error responses

4. **Document incident** (post-resolution)
   - Update incident log
   - Schedule post-mortem if P0/P1
   - Update runbooks if needed

### Escalation

| Severity | Escalation Path |
|----------|-----------------|
| P2 | Squad Fenix on-call |
| P1 | Squad Fenix lead + Platform team |
| P0 | VP Engineering + All hands |
```

### RB-010: Archive Data Retrieval (NEW)

```markdown
## Archive Data Retrieval

### Trigger
- LGPD data subject request
- Legal/compliance inquiry
- Audit request

### Access Control
Only the following roles can request archive retrieval:
- Data Protection Officer
- Legal Team
- Compliance Team

### Retrieval Steps

1. **Validate request** (1 day SLA)
   - Confirm requestor authorization
   - Verify CPF format
   - Log request for audit trail

2. **Determine data location**
   - If < 90 days: Query DynamoDB directly
   - If 90-365 days: Query S3 Standard/IA
   - If > 365 days: Initiate Glacier restore

3. **Glacier retrieval** (if applicable)
   ```bash
   # Initiate restore (Expedited: 1-5 min, Standard: 3-5 hours)
   aws s3api restore-object \
     --bucket fee-exemption-archive-123456789012-prod \
     --key "year=2025/month=01/cpf_token=abc123.parquet" \
     --restore-request '{"Days": 7, "GlacierJobParameters": {"Tier": "Standard"}}'
   ```

4. **Extract requested data**
   ```bash
   # Query with Athena (S3)
   aws athena start-query-execution \
     --query-string "SELECT * FROM fee_exemption_archive WHERE cpf_token = 'abc123'" \
     --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://query-results/
   ```

5. **Prepare response**
   - Decrypt CPF using KMS
   - Format according to LGPD requirements
   - Send via secure channel (never email)

6. **Log completion**
   - Record retrieval in audit log
   - Notify requestor
   - Delete temporary files
```

---

## Component Specifications

*(Retained from V2 with minor updates)*

### Lambda Functions Summary

| Function | Memory | Timeout | VPC | Concurrency |
|----------|--------|---------|-----|-------------|
| DLQ-Consumer | 256 MB | 30s | Yes | 5 (reserved) |
| Replay | 512 MB | 5 min | Yes | 10 (reserved) |
| Stuck Recovery | 256 MB | 60s | Yes | 1 |
| TTL Archiver | 256 MB | 60s | No | 5 |
| Health Check | 128 MB | 10s | Yes | 2 |

### Memory Tuning Guidance

Based on Lambda Power Tuning analysis:

| Function | Optimal Memory | Cost/Invocation | Duration (avg) |
|----------|----------------|-----------------|----------------|
| DLQ-Consumer | 256 MB | $0.000004 | 800ms |
| Replay | 512 MB | $0.000025 | 2.5s |
| Stuck Recovery | 256 MB | $0.000003 | 500ms |

## Appendix: Decision Log

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered |
|------|----------|-----------|------------------------|
| 2026-02-03 | Use DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless scaling, no connection pooling needed | Aurora Serverless (higher cost) |
| 2026-02-03 | CPF tokenization over encryption-only | Faster lookups without decryption | Field-level encryption (slower queries) |
| 2026-02-04 | Global Tables for DR | Automatic replication, <1min RPO | Manual cross-region backup (higher RPO) |
| 2026-02-04 | S3 for archival over DynamoDB | Cost-effective long-term storage | DynamoDB (10x cost for cold data) |
| 2026-02-04 | URI versioning over header | Simpler debugging, cache-friendly | Accept header (more RESTful but complex) |
| 2026-02-04 | Parquet for archives | Columnar format for efficient queries | JSON (simpler but larger) |

    17→
    18→## Executive Summary
    19→
    20→This document describes the **architecture solution** for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings. V5 is a refinement of V4 that **removes implementation-level details** (code, IAM policies, CloudFormation) to focus on architectural decisions. Implementation details will be addressed in the development phase.
    21→
    22→### Problem Statement
    23→
    24→When fee exemption promotions fail integration with CBILL (on-premises financial system), the current process requires:
    25→1. Manual identification of failed CPFs from DLQ
    26→2. Opening a ticket to the CBILL team for reprocessing
    27→3. No visibility into failure patterns or root causes
    28→
    29→**Constraints**:
    30→- DLQ has 14-day TTL - messages lost after this period
    31→- CBILL is on-premises with limited availability guarantees
    32→- CPF is PII requiring LGPD-compliant handling
    33→
    34→### Solution Overview
    35→
    36→Introduce a **persistent failure store** with **selective replay capability** and **automated retry scheduling**, eliminating manual intervention for common failure scenarios.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Architecture Diagram
    41→
    42→```
    43→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    44→│                              CURRENT FLOW                                        │
    45→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    46→│                                                                                  │
    47→│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
    48→│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
    49→│                         │                   │                                    │
    50→│                         │              [On Error]                                │
    51→│                         │                   │                                    │
    52→│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
    53→│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
    54→│                         │                                                        │
    55→│                         ▼                                                        │
    56→│                  Step Function ───► Email to Squad Fênix                        │
    57→│                                                                                  │
    58→│                  ❌ PROBLEM: Manual reprocessing via CBILL ticket               │
    59→│                                                                                  │
    60→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    61→
    62→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    63→│                              PROPOSED FLOW                                       │
    64→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    65→│                                                                                  │
    66→│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
    67→│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
    68→│                         │                   │                                    │
    69→│                         │              [On Error]                                │
    70→│                         │                   │                                    │
    71→│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
    72→│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
    73→│                         │                                                        │
    74→│            ┌────────────┴────────────┐                                          │
    75→│            ▼                         ▼                                          │
    76→│     DLQ-Consumer              Step Function                                     │
    77→│       Lambda                 (Email remains)                                    │
    78→│            │                                                                     │
    79→│            ▼                                                                     │
    80→│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │

    84→│   │  │ cpf_encrypted: KMS-encrypted CPF                 │   │                   │
    85→│   │  │ status: PENDING_RETRY | IN_PROGRESS | RESOLVED   │   │                   │
    86→│   │  │ error_code, retry_count, created_at, ttl         │   │                   │

    98→│            ▼                ▼                │                                  │
    99→│   ┌─────────────────────────────────┐       │                                  │
   100→│   │        Replay Lambda            │       │                                  │
   101→│   │  (Circuit Breaker Protected)    │◄──────┘                                  │
   102→│   └───────────────┬─────────────────┘       │                                  │
   103→│                   │                         │                                  │
   104→│                   ▼                         ▼                                  │
   105→│           SQS Main Queue             TTL Archiver                              │
   106→│           (Re-inject)                     │                                    │
